core_mechanism: Resonant coupling between the internal vibrational (normal-mode) degrees of freedom of interacting localized coherent structures and their translational center-of-mass motion, which renormalizes the effective pairwise interaction (even reversing its sign) and produces threshold barriers in configuration space where an internal-mode frequency crosses into/out of the continuum spectrum.
brief_reason: The abstract describes excited shape normal modes competing with the static inter-vortex force so that excited structures invert attraction/repulsion and encounter 'spectral walls' that block bound-state formation/break-up, i.e., internal oscillatory modes exchanging energy with translational motion to modify the effective force and erect frequency-threshold barriers.
